title: Latest News from Science Magazine
description: Keyword search result for Alerts: News Weekly Headlines -- New results matching your keyword search query (updated daily).

items.title: Researchers caught in the crossfire as companies and government grapple over AI safety
items.description: After tumultuous Fable 5 takedown, scientists fear AI safety rules could reshape open research

items.title: ‘Light in a bottle’ liquid can harvest and store energy from multiple sources
items.description: Substance’s “remarkable” behavior could lead to devices powered by energy-rich gels

items.title: New NIH security rules for genomic data sets are slowing research, prompting workarounds
items.description: Data security experts say increased oversight is needed, but researchers are struggling to comply

items.title: With new law, European Union can more quickly greenlight gene-edited crops
items.description: Final political hurdle overcome, easing regulation of certain modified plants

items.title: Speedy, spiraling electrical waves may be key to brain’s information flow
items.description: Rotating neuronal waves are built into brain anatomy and help coordinate far-flung regions, mouse imaging study suggests

items.title: Wolves are reconquering Europe. Can people learn to live with them?
items.description: As populations rebound, attacks on livestock and humans are fueling debate over the limits of coexistence

items.title: Do animals perceive time differently from humans?
items.description: <cite>Science</cite> chats with a researcher whose team is using “timescapes” to understand how nonhumans experience the world

items.title: A space telescope is falling to Earth. NASA is racing to rescue it
items.description: Vehicle will attempt a daring capture-and-boost mission to extend the life of the Swift observatory

items.title: Big Ebola outbreak puts spotlight on little-known virus
items.description: The Bundibugyo virus only emerged twice before. Now, scientists see a chance to get to know it better

items.title: The earliest plague epidemics may have been caused by marmots
items.description: Ancient hunter-gatherers may have contracted the pathogen from marmots
